## 3. Decisions (ADR Log)

<!-- Architecture Decision Records — log all significant decisions -->
<!-- Format: ADR-NNN: Title -->

| ID | Date | Decision | Status | Context |
|----|------|----------|--------|---------|
| ADR-001 | 2026-06-24 | Migrate via booting a real KK container per hop (Liquibase L1 + RealmMigration L2), not pure-SQL | Accepted | L2 RealmMigration runs only on server start; SQL export misses it |
| ADR-002 | 2026-06-24 | Hop path 16→24.0.5→26.6.3 (target 26) / 16→25.0.6 (target 25); forbid 26.6.0/26.6.1; PG≥14 for 26 | Accepted | Migrations cumulative (researched, 18 sources); fewer boots safer; avoid #48438/#47908 |
| ADR-003 | 2026-06-24 | KK images built FROM Astra/RedOS base; overridable image ref; offline tar load | Accepted | Sovereign OS requirement, air-gapped/banking delivery, branded images |
| ADR-004 | 2026-06-24 | CONTAINER_RUNTIME abstraction (podman/docker autodetect); single-host `run` topology added | Accepted | Astra/RedOS ship podman; tool was docker-hardcoded |
| ADR-005 | 2026-06-24 | Authoritative success gate = MIGRATION_MODEL advance (Layer 2), tightened wait + fail-closed | Accepted | "Container started" ≠ "migration applied"; old regex too loose |
| ADR-006 | 2026-06-26 | Air-gap bundle (extends ADR-003): build 8 images (16.1.1/24.0.5/25.0.6/26.6.3 × Astra SE/RED OS) FROM operator-supplied licensed bases → PRIVATE GHCR + PRIVATE Release tar; recipes/tooling PUBLIC, bases NEVER in repo; file-config (config/images.conf) for build/use/branded images | Accepted | Sovereign air-gap delivery + licence boundary; images can't live in git (size/licence) |
| ADR-007 | 2026-06-27 | Reduce manual setup: env-precedence for PROFILE_CONTAINER_IMAGE_REF/IMAGE_TAR/BASE_IMAGE in profile_load (use `<os>-<version>` tags directly, no re-tag); non-interactive `--yes`/`ASSUME_DEFAULTS` with a fail-closed main gate; one-shot `migrate_oneshot.sh`; config_wizard gains run+acquisition | Accepted | Real migration should be one command; the ':'-ref clobber forced a brittle re-tag; CI/automation need non-interactive runs without silently migrating prod |
| ADR-008 | 2026-07-14 | State is a FACT, not a journal: reconcile against reality (MIGRATION_MODEL version + DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK + live containers) BEFORE deciding hops; checkpoints and the profile's current_version are downgraded to claims/audit-trail | Accepted | Trusting the journal caused real damage on a live run: a stale checkpoint made the tool "skip the start" of a container that no longer existed and then wait out a 900s timeout; the profile's claimed current_version would also have re-run hops the DB had already passed |
| ADR-009 | 2026-07-14 | **Health is DIAGNOSTIC; L2 is the gate.** A hop's success is decided by MIGRATION_MODEL (ADR-005) and by nothing else. A failed/unreachable health probe produces a warning and never triggers a rollback; the rollback offer moves to the L2 gate alone and defaults to NO. `health_check` returns a three-valued code (OK / NOT_SERVED / UNCONFIRMED) and probes the port the version actually serves (KC≥25 → :9000/health/ready, KC<25 → :8080/health). Real post-migration acceptance moves to the `verify` subcommand | Accepted | The probe hardcoded `:8080/health`, which KC 24+ serves only with `KC_HEALTH_ENABLED=true` and KC≥25 moved to port 9000 — so it 404'd on **every** supported hop. A 404 was read as "unhealthy", and an unhealthy check rolled the migration back — over a hop L2 had already CONFIRMED. `_confirm`'s "Y" default auto-answers YES in any non-TTY (CI, cron, pipe) and under `--yes`, which `migrate_oneshot` always passes: silent, unattended restore-from-backup of a **successful** migration. `run` mode had an early `return 0` papering over it; the other five deployment modes carried the live defect |
<!-- | ADR-001 | 2026-01-15 | Use PostgreSQL for persistence | Accepted | Need ACID, complex queries | -->

## 6. Change Log

<!-- Significant architectural changes (auto-updated by Claude Code) -->

| Date | Change | ADR | Author |
|------|--------|-----|--------|
| 2026-06-24 | v3.7 container-hop migration: runtime abstraction, image acquisition (pull/load/preloaded/build), build helper, run topology, Layer-2 verification | ADR-001..005 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-06-26 | Air-gap packaging Phase A: Containerfile.kc16 (WildFly), build_matrix.sh (dry-run/build/publish + config/images.conf + USE-branded), build-images.yml (dispatch/self-hosted GHCR+Release), AIRGAP.md | ADR-006 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-06-27 | v3.9 automation: IMAGE_REF env-precedence (no re-tag), non-interactive `--yes`/`ASSUME_DEFAULTS` (fail-closed gate), `migrate_oneshot.sh`, config_wizard run+acquisition, profile_save acquisition/runtime, 5 new test suites | ADR-007 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-14 | v3.9.1 state reconciliation: `kc_reconcile_state()` reads MIGRATION_MODEL / DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK / live containers before planning hops; already-applied hops are skipped; stale Liquibase lock detected (`--force-unlock`); checkpoints verified against reality (`--no-resume`) | ADR-008 | @AlexGromer |
| 2026-07-14 | v3.9.2 the five deployment modes other than `run` never received the v3.9.1 fixes and carried data-loss defects: a 404 health probe rolled back an L2-confirmed migration (unattended, via `_confirm`'s "Y" default); checkpoints outlived the rollback that undid them; the docker/podman image update destroyed the user's container before discovering it could not rebuild it; hop backups were written to a directory rotation never swept; Ctrl-C left production stopped (`--replicas=0`) with nobody restoring it | ADR-009 | @AlexGromer |
<!-- | 2026-01-15 | Initial architecture | ADR-001 | @user | -->
